description_long: This object is a realistic depiction of a group of brown crabs, clustered together with overlapping bodies and limbs. The crabs have hard, oval-shaped carapaces with a slightly domed profile, and their surfaces are textured with natural ridges and subtle color variations. Each crab features prominent, elongated claws (chelae) that are slightly open and positioned forward, as well as multiple segmented walking legs radiating outward from the body. The coloration is predominantly a warm, earthy brown with hints of orange and darker shading along the joints and edges. The crabs' eyes are not clearly visible, and their antennae are either absent or not discernible in these views. The overall arrangement suggests a pile of crabs, as might be seen in a seafood market or restaurant display.
description: A cluster of brown crabs with hard, textured shells and prominent claws, arranged in a natural pile with overlapping legs and bodies.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crabs' overlapping claws and carapaces, with most legs splayed outward and the pile's layered structure most apparent.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the domed shape of the carapaces and the segmentation of the legs, with claws partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view emphasizing the rounded backs of the carapaces and the spread of the walking legs, with claws less prominent.
description_view_3: Side view showing the stacked arrangement of the crabs, with legs and claws extending outward and the layered bodies visible.
